examples.title: Demonstrating Race Condition with Shared Variable
examples.description: Code showing incorrect parallelization due to shared variable causing data race
examples.explanation: The statement 'counter++' is not atomic. It involves three operations: (1) load counter from memory to register, (2) increment register, (3) store register back to memory. When multiple threads execute concurrently, they may read the same value before any writes complete, causing lost updates. This is a classic race condition.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p) where n=8000 iterations, p=8 threads

examples.title: Fixing Race Condition with Reduction Clause
examples.description: Corrected version using reduction to safely accumulate results from multiple threads
examples.explanation: The reduction clause creates a private copy of 'counter' for each thread, initialized to 0 (identity for +). Each thread increments its private copy without any synchronization. At the end of the parallel for, the OpenMP runtime combines all private copies using the + operator, ensuring correct final result. No race condition exists because there's no concurrent access to shared memory.

examples.title: Demonstrating Firstprivate and Lastprivate
examples.description: Code showing how firstprivate initializes and lastprivate preserves final values
examples.explanation: firstprivate(initial) copies the value 100 to each thread's private copy before parallel execution. Each thread modifies its private copy during iterations, but the original 'initial' variable remains unchanged (100). lastprivate(result) copies the value of 'result' from the last sequential iteration (i=9) back to the original 'result' variable after the parallel region. The final value printed will be 218 (from iteration 9: (100+9)*2).
